criteria.label: Write tests in the AAA pattern with full-sentence names describing the behaviour.
criteria.example: `test_chunker_respects_max_tokens()` with explicit Arrange / Act / Assert comments.

criteria.label: Use `pytest.raises` for expected exceptions; never bare try/except.
criteria.example:
```python
with pytest.raises(ValueError, match='max_tokens must be positive'):
    chunk_text('hi', -1)
```

criteria.label: Parametrize tables of inputs instead of copy-pasting test bodies.
Keep the full suite under ~30 seconds by mocking I/O and avoiding real `time.sleep`.
criteria.example: Use `freezegun` for timeouts/backoff; `unittest.mock.patch` for API clients; SQLite `:memory:` for DBs when needed.

criteria.label: Add a regression test for every bug fix; reference the bug ID in a comment.
criteria.example:
```python
# regression test for #421 — chunker dropped final chunk on exact boundary
def test_chunker_keeps_final_chunk_on_exact_boundary(): ...
```

criteria.label: Assert on observable behaviour, not private implementation details.
criteria.example: Test `chunker.chunks(text)` returns expected list; don't assert on `chunker._buffer`.
